title: Trade Wins: The Ranat's Tale

"'''Trade Wins: The Ranat's Tale'''" is a short story written by [[Rebecca Moesta]] for ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', which was published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[August]] [[1995]]. The story is about [[Reegesk/Legends|Reegesk]] and takes place in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

==Plot summary==
[[Reegesk/Legends|Reegesk]] enters the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley Cantina]] looking to [[Merchant/Legends|trade]] away his items in his search for a power source for a bootleg [[Moisture vaporator/Legends|vaporator]] his tribe is secretly building in order to have less dependence on [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmers]]. He finds customer in [[Het Nkik/Legends|Het Nkik]] who, through the bartering process, shows a keen interest in the [[Ranat/Legends|Ranat's]] [[Tusken battle talisman]], examining it carefully with his hands. In return Reegesk wants Nkik's [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. The [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]], however, is reluctant to give up his [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]], carefully stating that he cannot afford the price of talisman until the following [[Standard day/Legends|day]] but gives a deposit in [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] after bartering a higher price than Reegesk expected.

Reegesk decides to hand the talisman over in the morning, but Nkik states that he needs that artifact that day; tomorrow he will pay the rest of the agreed price ''and'' give the Ranat the [[Pistol/Legends|pistol]]. Though arming Ranats in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] is illegal, the Jawa places the weapon down for Reegesk to inspect. As Reegesk touches the gun, the two are suddenly distracted by the sound of a blaster bolt from across the room and see [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]] slumped over a table. Reegesk agrees to the deal, bringing back Nkik's attention from the [[Death/Legends|dead]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who then pulls the blaster away from the Ranat before leaving silently with confidence. Moments later, Reegesk exits the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] content that he now has the perfect power supply for his tribe's vaporator, and pats the stolen [[Power cell/Legends|power pack]] in his [[Cloak/Legends|cloak]].

==Continuity==
The short story [[Swap Meet: The Jawa's Tale|''Swap Meet'']] ended with a cliffhanger that is not resolved until the reader completes ''Trade Wins'', as the stories were meant to be read back-to-back. [[Het Nkik/Legends|Het Nkik's]] story also intersected with [[When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|''When the Desert Wind Turns'']] and [[At the Crossroads: The Spacer's Tale|''At the Crossroads'']], and was referenced in [[Drawing the Maps of Peace: The Moisture Farmer's Tale|''Drawing the Maps of Peace'']] and [[Spare Parts (short story)|''Spare Parts'']].<ref name="TERC" />
